Your LADWP bill is not just about how much air conditioning you run.

The windows in your home play a major role in how hard your cooling system has to work. In Los Angeles, that matters even more during the long warm season.

In our latest Home Insights Blog, we explain:
• how older windows can increase heat gain
• why energy-efficient windows can improve comfort
• what the savings data may mean for Southern California homeowners
• when replacement may be a better investment than repair

If you have noticed drafty rooms, rising energy bills, or windows that no longer seem to perform well, this is a helpful place to start.

Read the blog at constructionconcern.com

If one room in your home always feels hotter, draftier, or louder than the rest, your windows may be part of the problem.

A few common signs:
* indoor temperatures shift quickly
* drafts near frames
* increased outside noise
* windows that stick, fog, or feel worn

Window replacement is not only about appearance. It can also improve comfort and everyday livability.
